cards.a: The real goal of the business (sell, generate leads, build brand). The plan starts here.
Awareness, Traffic, Engagement, Leads, App Promotion, Sales.
Where the conversion happens: Website, App, Messaging, Calls, Instant form.
The action that counts as a result: Purchase, Lead, AddToCart, etc.
Determines what outcome an advertiser is trying to accomplish based on the objective, optimization and other offsite signals they provide in ad setup.
A piece of code installed on a website that tracks website events.

An API that enables developers to send website event data to Meta directly from a server, as opposed to sending the data through the Meta Pixel (which uses a website browser). This data is then connected to a pixel ID and can be used for various purposes, from measurement to optimization.
An authorized partner for measuring in-app events (an alternative to the Facebook SDK).
The method by which an advertiser buys ads across Meta technologies: Auction or Reservation.
An ad buying type wherein the advertiser decides to participate in the ad auction: more objectives, 5 bid strategies, all placements; variable price.
An ad buying type that enables advertisers to reach an established number of people within an audience at a specified frequency at a fixed price (CPM); only Awareness and Engagement.
Advertiser bid + estimated action rates + ad quality. Cost control does NOT factor in.
A calculated value determined by how likely it is that an ad will lead to a desired outcome after it's shown to a person.
A calculated value determined by the quality of ad creative along with how relevant an ad is to the chosen individual.
Understand the client's business goal, not pick an objective in Ads Manager.
Business goal → funnel stage → objective → conversion location → event → performance goal.
Top: Awareness/Traffic · Mid: Engagement/Leads · Bottom: Sales/App Promotion.
Objective = campaign level. Conversion location, event and performance goal = ad set level.

cards.q: Maximize number vs value of conversions
cards.a: Number = more units. Value = higher amount per conversion (requires value tracking).
Meta Pixel + Conversions API (CAPI) together.
Facebook SDK or an authorized MMP.
Requires no Pixel; the contact is captured within Meta.
Awareness and Engagement (exactly the only ones Reservation supports).

cards.q: Trap: wanting sales and choosing Traffic
cards.a: Traffic optimizes for link clicks, not purchases. Real sales = Sales.
A spend-based bid strategy that maximizes delivery and the number of results you get with your budget.
A spend-based bid strategy that prioritizes spending the budget and focusing on highest value purchases.
A goal-based bid strategy that enables advertisers to provide a benchmark cost for the results they care about, keeping a target cost per result.
A goal-based bid strategy in which the system tries to deliver against a target return on ad spend over the campaign's lifetime.
A bid strategy that lets the advertiser set a maximum bid to be used in each auction. If set too low, it slows delivery.
Average spend per day; for ongoing campaigns.
Total for the period; required for ad scheduling and Reservation.
A setting in Ads Manager that distributes spend across the ad sets in a campaign to get the best overall performance.
A fixed budget per ad set; manual distribution.

In Ads Manager, this is when information is being collected to optimize ad delivery (~50 events in ~7 days); before that, results are unstable. This term refers to machine learning, not advertiser learning.
Estimate: target results × expected cost per result.
Calculation: impressions × CPM ÷ 1000 (impressions = reach × frequency).
Recommended approach: your audiences are suggestions, not limits; Meta searches for conversions.
An audience of people on Meta technologies who have already interacted with or shown interest in a brand: web, app, customer list, engagement.
A group of people who are similar to a quality seed audience; for prospecting. 1% = more precise, 10% = more reach.
An audience so narrow that the system can't optimize: low delivery, high CPM.
Spend-based (Volume/Value), goal-based (Cost per Result/ROAS Goal), manual (Bid Cap).
Highest Volume / Highest Value if there are no strict cost goals.

cards.q: Risk of a miscalibrated Bid Cap
cards.a: A cap set too low: the ad barely enters auctions and the campaign doesn't spend.
500,000 people × frequency 3 × CPM $4 ÷ 1000 = ~$6,000 USD.
400 leads × CPL $5 ≈ ~$2,000 USD (estimate, not a guarantee).
Up to ~25% day to day, respecting the period's average.
Website, App activity, Customer list, Engagement.
Remarketing, lookalike seed and excluding people who already converted.
An audience option that enables advertisers to reach people based on demographics, location, interests and behaviors; for cold audiences without first-party signal.
Allows Meta to go beyond the set interest if it improves results (within the original audience).
Where ads appear on a website or app across Meta technologies, such as Feed, Instagram Stories or inbox in Messenger.
An option in Ads Manager that shows an ad on the widest range of available placements across Meta technologies; the recommended default for more reach and efficiency.
Automatic creative optimizations per person (crops, music, text).
The causal effect of the ads: conversions that would NOT have happened without the ads.
A test that uses randomized control trials (RCTs) to measure the number of incremental conversions that happen as a result of ads across Meta technologies.
Also known as brand affinity, a metric that provides insight into people's brand awareness and attitudes toward a brand (exposed vs. control survey).
A test that uses a control and test group to compare multiple ad sets and see which performs the best.
The group of people in a test who aren't exposed to ads. Control groups are used as a benchmark to measure the effect of the test.
A measurement solution that enables you to see which app, channel, device, ads or content should be credited with a conversion; it does not prove causality on its own.
The period of time during which conversions can be credited to a particular ad (e.g. 7-day click / 1-day view).
Models the aggregate impact of all channels (online/offline) at a macro level.
Lists are uploaded hashed; Meta matches without receiving data in the clear.
A control over the suitability of adjacent content (expanded / moderate / limited).
Block = exclude specific publishers. Allow = permit only approved publishers.
Review of the ad (text, creative, targeting, landing) against the Advertising Standards (~24 h).
It is prohibited to imply or assert that you know a personal attribute of the user or their family.
Only with a real business reason: a brand requirement or unsuitable creative. Not for aesthetics.

cards.q: Effect of restricting placements without reason
cards.a: Raises cost per result and reduces delivery.
Raises estimated action rates and ad quality (total value) → lower cost per result.
Design vertical 9:16 without relying on sound; brand early; multiple formats.
Several creatives per ad set: more optimization space and less fatigue.
Awareness=recall/reach · Traffic=clicks/CPC · Leads/Sales=cost per result · Revenue=ROAS.
Likes/impressions/clicks that look good but don't prove the business goal.
Combine experiments + attribution + MMM for a robust read.
A requirement to collect and use data in advertising.
It is prohibited to target using inferred sensitive categories (health, religion, etc.).
Stricter = safer but less reach and possibly higher cost.
A rejected ad can be edited or appealed; edits go back to ad review.

items.d: First you define the business goal (awareness, leads, sales) and from there you derive the correct ODAX campaign objective. The objective isn't chosen at random: it responds to the goal and the funnel stage.
A plan is built by funnel phases (awareness → consideration → conversion), assigning objective, audience, budget and measurement to each phase, not everything to direct conversion.

items.t: Conversion location, event and performance goal
items.d: Conversion location = where it happens (web, app, messages...). Conversion event = what counts (Purchase, Lead...). Performance goal = how it's optimized/bid. These are three distinct decisions.
Maximum reach of people who will recall the ad. Top of funnel.
Send people to a destination: web, app, call or messages.
Interaction with the business: messages, video views, post engagement.
Collect contacts via forms, calls or sign-ups.
Install the app or increase its usage.
People more likely to buy. Requires a conversion signal (Pixel/CAPI).
Daily: average spend per day, even delivery. Lifetime: total for the entire period, allows scheduling/dayparting and variable distribution by day.
Campaign budget (CBO): a campaign-level budget that the system distributes dynamically to the best-performing ad set. ABO: a fixed budget per ad set (manual control).
In Auction the cost is set by the auction (variable). In Reservation you buy a fixed CPM in advance: budget ≈ reach × frequency × CPM, predictable.
Auction: flexible, optimizes by auction, all objectives. Reservation: predictable delivery and CPM bought in advance (TV-style planning), limited objectives.
Highest Volume · Highest Value · Cost per Result Goal · ROAS Goal · Bid Cap. Spend-based ones spend the whole budget; goal-based ones respect a cost/return goal; Bid Cap is manual. Auction only.
Targeting by demographics, interests and behaviors to define a 'core' audience. Very small audiences limit exiting the learning phase.
People who already interacted with you: customer list (hashed), web/app traffic, engagement on Meta, video viewers. The basis for retargeting and Lookalikes.
People similar to a quality source. 1%–10% of the country: 1% = more similar/less reach, 10% = more reach/less precise.
Automates audience selection: your inputs are a guide, not a limit; the system explores beyond them to find who converts.
Advantage+ placements (recommended): the system delivers where it performs best at lower cost. Manual: you choose, useful only with a clear strategic reason.
9:16 vertical full screen (Stories/Reels) · 1:1 square (feed) · 4:5 mobile feed · 16:9 horizontal. Design mobile-first and adapt the creative to the placement.
Default window 7-day click + 1-day view. Defines which conversions get credit; affects reporting.
Compares isolated variables with mutually exclusive audiences to find a statistically significant winner.
Brand Lift: surveys, measures recall/awareness (upper funnel). Conversion Lift: holdout, measures real incremental conversion (lower funnel).
The additional value the advertising generated vs what would have happened without it (measured with a control group). It's not the same as attributed conversions.
ATT reduced iOS signal. Aggregated Event Measurement: max 8 prioritized events per verified domain; prioritize the main conversion event.
Brand Safety Center: Expanded (default, more reach) · Moderate · Limited (more exclusion, less reach).
Allow excluding content types or sensitive topics. Topic exclusions apply only to Facebook in-stream and reels.
Use only data with consent; customer lists are uploaded hashed. Privacy protects the user's and their family's personal information.
